title: Auto-linking (and summing) data across multiple boards without mirroring notes
excerpt: I’ve been trying to crack this for a while now and have consistently come up empty. What I’m trying to do involves sharing data (some of it raw, some of it summed) across multiple boards that use different titles as item…

posts.author: mattpointr (Matt Clough)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve been trying to crack this for a while now and have consistently come up empty. What I’m trying to do involves sharing data (some of it raw, some of it summed) across multiple boards that use different titles as items - but achieving this without having the notes/ updates made to the linked items copy over to one another.

The use case looks like this:

  • We have a deals board, with a connected (single direction) column to a companies board that is always filled in. Each deal has a value
  • I want to automatically pull a sum of the values for all deals associated with a specific company back to the company board, and list the value in a column
  • This can be achieved by setting up two way connection columns between the two boards, and then using a mirror column on the companies board to sum the associated value of all the related deals
  • However, doing this means that every update/ note/ activity logged to a deal is also recorded against the company, and vice versa - this is what I’m trying to avoid, as we tend to have 10+ deals per company and it will just mean both company and deal logs will become clogged with irrelevant updates

Any help would be hugely appreciated!

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml: @mattpointr when you use a mirror column it only mirrors the specific column you choose. If you select a numbers column to sum, it will only show that without all the updates etc.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: I think they are discussing the Emails & Activities, which yes has issues with multiple items/deals etc.

posts.author: garfield-labs (Snir Alon)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Matt, Same Item Multiple Boards can do that. You can also block updates from being synced, here’s a tutorial: How to Hide Updates from Other Boards | Same Item Multiple Boards

posts.author: mattpointr (Matt Clough)
posts.cookedHtml: @Fantasy-Media-Team @anon29275264 thank you for your replies - Cody is correct, I have no problems with the way the mirror columns themselves work, they’re ideal for what I’m trying to do. The issue I’m having is that in order to create the mirror columns based on a two-way connection, it then means that there’s a knock-on effect on every item that’s connected picking up shared items when viewing the individual item cards.
